content.cn_text: 大家早上好。上一讲中，我们讨论了工程设计中的创新，
content.en_text: Good morning, everyone. In the last lecture we discussed innovation in engineering design,

content.cn_text: 今天我想来看一个案例研究。我将讲一个工程项目，
content.en_text: and today I want to look at a case study. I'll be talking about an engineering project

content.cn_text: 这个项目最近在苏格兰开展，目的是修复两条旧水道，实际上是运河，
content.en_text: which was recently carried out in Scotland to restore two old waterways, canals in fact,

content.cn_text: 然后把它们连接起来。我会谈到它的设计特点，但首先我会告诉
content.en_text: and then link them together. I'll talk about the design features, but first I'll tell

content.cn_text: 你们一点这个项目的历史背景。其中一条运河叫作
content.en_text: you a little bit about the historical background of the project. One of these canals called

content.cn_text: 福斯-克莱德运河，于1790年开通，它是苏格兰最古老的运河。另一条，
content.en_text: the Forth and Clyde opened in 1790, and it's the oldest of Scotland's canals. The other,

content.cn_text: 联合运河于1822年开通。虽然这两条运河建在不同高度，
content.en_text: the Union Canal opened in 1822. Although the two canals were built at different levels,

content.cn_text: 但它们共同使船只能够从西向东穿越苏格兰中部。这是因为
content.en_text: together they enabled vessels to travel from west to east across central Scotland. This is

content.cn_text: 这两条运河在一个叫福尔柯克的小镇附近，由一套复杂的船闸系统连接起来，
content.en_text: because the canals were connected near a town called Falkirk by a complicated system of locks,

content.cn_text: 船闸有阶梯和闸门，使船只能够从一个高度移动到另一个高度。然而，
content.en_text: which have steps and gates allowing boats to move from one level to another. However,

content.cn_text: 从1842年起，当服务于同一条
content.en_text: from 1842, the two canals faced serious competition when the railway opened which served the same

content.cn_text: 横贯路线的铁路开通后，这两条运河面临严重竞争。此后，它们的使用量稳步下降，到了20世纪30年代，
content.en_text: cross-country route. After that, their use declined steadily, and in the 1930s the locks at

content.cn_text: 福尔柯克的船闸被拆除了。然而时代在变化。现在说到千禧年之交，
content.en_text: Falkirk were demolished. Times change, however. Moving on now to the turn of the millennium,

content.cn_text: 苏格兰启动了一个雄心勃勃的新项目。目标是升级该国的基础设施，
content.en_text: Scotland embarked on an ambitious new project. The aim was to upgrade the country's infrastructure,

content.cn_text: 尤其是在交通领域。这个项目有数百万英镑的预算。
content.en_text: especially in the area of transport. There was a multi-million pound budget for this.

content.cn_text: 这确实是一项非常大的投资。事实上，整个项目只是一个长期
content.en_text: It was a very big investment indeed. And in fact, the whole project was just one part of a long

content.cn_text: 计划的一部分，该计划旨在刺激国家经济。除此之外，
content.en_text: term plan which had been developed to stimulate the national economy. And amongst other things,

content.cn_text: 它还包括修复这两条旧运河，并使其重新投入使用。然而，
content.en_text: it involved restoring the two old canals and bringing them back into use. However, there was

content.cn_text: 正如我刚才提到的，这里有一个重大挑战。福斯-克莱德运河位于
content.en_text: one major challenge as I mentioned just now. The Forth and Clyde Canal lies below the level

content.cn_text: 联合运河的水平面之下。两者之间有35米的高度差，而船闸已经被毁。
content.en_text: of the Union Canal. There's a 35-meter difference, and the lock had been destroyed.

content.cn_text: 所以问题是如何让船只从一个高度到达另一个高度。无论选择哪种解决方案，
content.en_text: So the problem was how to get boats from one level to the other. Whatever kind of solution was chosen,

content.cn_text: 人们都认为它应该真正具有创新性和想象力。这是纪念
content.en_text: it was felt that it should be really innovative and imaginative. A fitting way to mark the

content.cn_text: 新千禧年开始的一种合适方式。最终建成的结构是一个船只机械升降装置，
content.en_text: beginning of a new millennium. The resulting structure was a mechanical lift for boats,

content.cn_text: 因为它会旋转，所以被称为福尔柯克轮。福尔柯克轮于
content.en_text: and because it rotates, it was called the Falkirk Wheel. The Falkirk Wheel was completed

content.cn_text: 2002年完工。它是同类结构中的首创，是一项工程创新。该结构
content.en_text: in 2002. And it was the first structure of its kind, an engineering innovation. The structure

content.cn_text: 由两个非常大的水槽组成，事实上每个水槽都足以容纳一艘船。它们被固定
content.en_text: consists of two very large tanks, each large enough to contain a boat, in fact. These are fixed

content.cn_text: 在一条垂直臂的两端。基本上，它的运作方式是：这条臂把一艘船从
content.en_text: onto opposite sides of a vertical arm. Basically, what happens is that the arm lowers one boat from

content.cn_text: 较高的运河降到下面的运河。同时，它又把另一艘船从较低的
content.en_text: the higher canal to the canal below. And at the same time, it raises another boat from the lower

content.cn_text: 运河提升到上方运河的高度。这是如何运作的呢？水槽的安装方式
content.en_text: canal to the level of the upper canal. How does this work? Well, the tanks are mounted in such a way

content.cn_text: 使它们保持在一个水平平面上。当一艘船连同承载它漂浮的水
content.en_text: that they stay in a horizontal plane. As one boat, together with the water that it floats in,

content.cn_text: 驶入上方水槽时，另一艘漂浮的船同时进入下方水槽。
content.en_text: sails into the upper tank, another floating boat simultaneously enters the lower tank.

content.cn_text: 这个系统基于科学原理运作，这些原理确保每艘船的重量加上
content.en_text: The system works on scientific principles, which ensure that the weight of each boat plus

content.cn_text: 承载它漂浮的水的重量会与另一边相等。所以，一旦船只进入水槽，
content.en_text: the water that it floats in will be equal to the other. So, once the boats have entered the tank,

content.cn_text: 这条臂就会旋转。由于水槽处于平衡状态，只需要非常少的能量
content.en_text: the arm rotates. And because the tanks are in balance, only a very small amount of energy is

content.cn_text: 就能完成这种旋转。工程师们普遍认为福尔柯克轮非常实用。
content.en_text: required for this rotation. Engineers generally agree that the Falkirk Wheel is highly functional.

content.cn_text: 它在视觉上也非常惊艳。它的形状不同寻常，有许多曲线，看起来很有吸引力。
content.en_text: It's also visually stunning. It's an unusual shape. It looks very attractive with a lot of curves.

content.cn_text: 事实上，它几乎看起来像一件雕塑。那么，福尔柯克轮是在哪里制造的呢？
content.en_text: In fact, it almost looks like a sculpture. So, where was the Falkirk Wheel manufactured?

content.cn_text: 它完全是在场外制造的，地点在英格兰，距离约370公里。
content.en_text: Well, it was produced entirely off-site at a place in England about 370 kilometers away.

content.cn_text: 它由钢材建造，用了1200吨钢。它也非常坚固。例如，
content.en_text: It was constructed from steel, 1,200 tons of it. It was also very strong. For example,

content.cn_text: 接头不仅用螺栓固定，还进行了焊接。这是因为大量压力
content.en_text: the joints were bolted together as well as being welded. This was because a lot of stress would

content.cn_text: 会由船只和水的重量施加在这些接头上。工程中使用了超过15000个螺栓，
content.en_text: be placed on them by the weight of the boats and the water. Over 15,000 bolts were used and

content.cn_text: 并且每一个都是手工拧紧的。当然，自从投入使用以来，这一设计一直在
content.en_text: each one was tightened by hand. Of course, since it's been in use, the design has been tested on

content.cn_text: 持续接受测试。而它可能会……
content.en_text: an ongoing basis. And it may be...

groups.desc: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.title: Questions 31–40

items.title: 1790: opening of the Forth and Clyde Canal (Scotland’s 31. __________ canal)
items.analysis: 答案解析：第31题问 Forth and Clyde Canal 是 Scotland’s _______ canal，原文说 it’s the oldest of Scotland’s canals，因此答案是 oldest。

items.title: The two canals allowed boats to cross the 32. __________ part of Scotland
items.analysis: 答案解析：第32题问 boats could cross the _______ part of Scotland，原文说 across central Scotland，因此答案是 central。

items.title: 1842: competition from a new 33. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析：第33题问 1842 年出现了来自新 _______ 的竞争，原文说 the railway opened，因此答案是 railway。

items.title: Aim: to improve Scotland’s 34. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析：第34题问 aim 是 improve Scotland’s _______，原文说 especially in the area of transport，因此答案是 transport。

items.title: The long-term goal – to strengthen the Scottish 35. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析：第35题问 long-term goal 是 strengthen the Scottish _______，原文说 stimulate the national economy，因此答案是 economy。

items.title: The tanks are designed to remain in a 36. __________ position
items.analysis: 答案解析：第36题问 tanks remain in a _______ position，原文说 stay in a horizontal plane，因此答案是 horizontal。

items.title: The weight of the tanks with their contents is always 37. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析：第37题问 tanks with contents 的重量总是 _______，原文说 will be equal to the other，因此答案是 equal。

items.title: Visual quality – resembles a 38. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析：第38题问 visual quality resembles a _______，原文说 looks like a sculpture，因此答案是 sculpture。

items.title: 1,200 tonnes of 39. __________ were used
items.analysis: 答案解析：第39题问 1,200 tonnes of _______ were used，原文说 constructed from steel，因此答案是 steel。

items.title: Joints were bolted and welded to reduce the effects of 40. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析：第40题问 bolted and welded 是为了 reduce the effects of _______，原文说 a lot of stress would be placed on them，因此答案是 stress。

groups.source_collect:
Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
New engineering designs: Scottish case study
Project to renovate and link two canals in Scotland
Historical background
• 1790: opening of the Forth and Clyde Canal (Scotland’s $31$ canal)
• 1822: opening of the Union Canal
• The two canals allowed boats to cross the $32$ part of Scotland
• Locks (steps) connected the two canals near Falkirk
• 1842: competition from a new $33$
• 1930s: the locks were demolished
Millennium project
• Aim: to improve Scotland’s $34$
• The long-term goal – to strengthen the Scottish $35$
• Involved restoration of the two canals
• Problem: one canal is 35m lower than the other
The Falkirk Wheel (a mechanical lift)
• Completed in 2002
• Two large tanks are attached to the ends of an arm
• The tanks are designed to remain in a $36$ position
• A boat sails into each tank
• The weight of the tanks with their contents is always $37$
• Little energy is needed to rotate the arm
• Visual quality – resembles a $38$
Manufacturing
• All the parts were constructed off-site
• 1,200 tonnes of $39$ were used
• Joints were bolted and welded to reduce the effects of $40$
